extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won stage 7 in a photo finish over BOASSON HAGEN Edvald by millimeters after trailing until the final half-foot.
tactical_patterns: Kittel positioned third wheel when Sabatini dropped him 5km out, conserving energy while rivals burned matches leading early.
Van Rensburg's Dimension Data leadout for Boasson Hagen matched QuickStep's quality, revealing team adapted post-Cavendish crash tactics immediately.
Boasson Hagen took shortest line around right bend 180m out, forcing Kittel wider but losing momentum from early sprint initiation.
Sky kept Froome near front throughout finale despite non-GC stage, prioritizing crash avoidance over energy conservation on wind-threatened course.

rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: positive: sprinted from 180m out against pure sprinters, suggesting deeper speed than expected for classics-type rider
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: averaged 42.3mph final 500m while coming from behind, indicating superior top-end acceleration when positioned correctly
rider_signals.VAN RENSBURG Jaco: positive: delivered Boasson Hagen perfectly positioned at 180m, demonstrating Dimension Data retained elite leadout capacity post-Cavendish
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: crash exit revealed in commentary context, removing primary Dimension Data sprint option early in Tour
